title: Selectors, Cardinality, Unknown Values, and Typed Extraction
description: A broad selector takes the first node, missing and empty values collapse together, strings are cast into domain types, and markup drift silently corrupts output. Learners cumulatively produce runtime-validated extraction contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Define selector scope, expected cardinality, fallback order, exclusions, and source location before extracting.
Represent absent, empty, malformed, duplicate, conflicting, and admitted values as distinct outcomes.
Keep attributes, text, JSON-LD, configuration, and plugin output unknown until validators produce domain values.
